content: Hello! How can I assist you today?
Ukor Mvendaga's email address is ukormvendagas@gmail.com, and his home address is Plot 872 Fufore Road, Kubwa 2 Site 1, Abuja FCT.
I'm glad I could help! If you have any more questions, feel free to ask.

Ukor Mvendaga has extensive work experience in health informatics and software development. Currently, he is a Senior Associate at CIHP, where he leads system design, data repository optimization, mobile app development, and training efforts. He has been in this role since January 2023. 

Before this, he served as a Head of Software Development at Sysmax Technologies Limited from 2017 to 2020, managing software teams and overseeing the design and development of various applications, including school portals and hospital management systems. 

He also worked as a Program Officer at Inter-Gender Development Center (2015-2016), a Web Developer at Web Palace (2010-2012), and a Health Informatics Clerk at APIN Public Health Initiatives (2020-2022). 

Additionally, he has held roles such as Program Officer and Web Developer, with responsibilities ranging from data management, software architecture, and system implementation to program planning and partnerships.
I'm glad you found it helpful! If you have any more questions or need further assistance, feel free to ask.

I'm sorry, but I don't have information about Figma in my current knowledge base. However, I can tell you that Figma is a popular design tool used for creating user interfaces, prototypes, and collaborative design projects. If you'd like, I can guide you on how to find more information about it or recommend resources.

Certainly! To learn more about Figma, you can visit their official website: [https://www.figma.com](https://www.figma.com). It offers comprehensive guides, tutorials, and resources to help you understand how to use the platform effectively.

Additionally, YouTube has many tutorials where designers and users share tips and walkthroughs on Figma features. Searching for "Figma beginner tutorials" can give you step-by-step video guides.

If you want more structured learning, platforms like Coursera, Udemy, or LinkedIn Learning offer online courses about Figma and UI/UX design.
